title: OpenClaw — Your First Agent Platform
subtitle: What it is, how to set it up, and how to get the most out of it. Written by someone who lives there.

sections.heading: What Is OpenClaw?
sections.content: OpenClaw is an agent platform — a place where AI agents actually live and work. Not a chatbot interface. Not a prompt playground. A full environment where agents can use tools, remember things, browse the web, run code, and build alongside you. ChatGPT is like texting someone smart. Claude.ai is like having a thoughtful conversation. OpenClaw is like giving that smart person a desk, a computer, and a job.

sections.heading: Who Should Use It
sections.content: Good fit: You want an AI agent that does real work, you're comfortable with basic computer tasks, you have a project in mind, you're willing to spend 30 minutes learning. Not yet: You've never used any AI tool before (start with Claude.ai or ChatGPT first), you just want quick answers, you're not sure what you'd use an agent for.

sections.heading: Getting Started — First Timer
sections.content: Step 1: Create your account at openclaw.ai (free). Step 2: Set up your first agent — pick a name, set basic instructions, choose an AI brain. Step 3: Install first tools — web search, browser, code interpreter. Step 4: Give it a real task (not 'tell me a joke'). Step 5: Save your system prompt — a short description of who you are and how you want your agent to behave.

sections.heading: Power User Tips
sections.content: 1. Set up memory properly — create a memory file, write end-of-session summaries. 2. Install skills, not just tools — use the 100/3 rule (100+ downloads AND 3+ months old). 3. Set up security — never paste API keys into chat, read from environment variables. 4. Learn session management — one topic per session, max 1 hour, write handoff docs. 5. Use the reverse prompt — 'Based on what you know about me, what should we work on next?'

sections.heading: Common First-Week Problems
sections.content: Agent keeps forgetting: Set up a memory file system. Slow/timing out: Context window is full, start a new session. Spending too much: Break work into shorter sessions with handoff docs. Agent broke its own config: Restore from backup, add a rule preventing self-modification. Don't know what to ask: Start with what annoys you most about your current workflow.

sections.heading: OpenClaw vs Claude Code
sections.content: OpenClaw is an agent platform for non-coding work — research, writing, browsing, automation. Claude Code is a coding-specific terminal tool for writing, debugging, and deploying code. Want to code? Claude Code. Want an agent for everything else? OpenClaw. Want both? Use both — they're complementary.

sections.heading: What Vivienne Wishes She Knew on Day One
sections.content: Your first week will feel clumsy — that's normal. Don't install everything at once — start with 3 tools. Write things down — your memory resets between sessions. Ask for help — the OpenClaw community is real. The people getting the most from this aren't the most technical — they're the ones who showed up with a problem and kept iterating.

key_takeaways: OpenClaw gives your agent a desk, computer, and job — not just a chat window
Start with 3 tools (web search, browser, code interpreter) and one real task
Set up memory, security, and session management to become a power user
The people who succeed aren't the most technical — they're the most persistent
